title: lavandula angustifolia f. albiflora
intro: Lavandula angustifolia f. albiflora, also known as white lavender, is a variety of lavender plant characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers. This plant is valued for its calming and soothing properties, and it is used to treat a range of health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The medicinal properties of white lavender include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which make it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. White lavender is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and potpourri, and it is often used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and reduce stress levels.
